channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bragg pitches post-presidency Trump sentencing in renewed push urging Judge Merchan to keep conviction alive
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg's office called on Judge Juan Merchan to not drop President-elect Donald Trump's Manhattan criminal conviction, offering alternative options in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle gets Greece as consolation prize after Donald Trump Jr. dumps her: Source
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle has been tapped to be the U.S. Ambassador to Greece in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Outgoing Treasury Sec. Yellen 'sorry that we haven't made more progress,' believes deficit must be decreased
item.description: Treasury Sec. Janet Yellen, who will leave office next month, discussed the deficit at the Wall Street Journal's CEO Council Summit, noting that she's "concerned about fiscal sustainability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DFL, Republican senators weigh in on state budget forecast
item.description: The state’s latest economic forecast, revealed Dec. 4, predicts less revenue than expected and higher-than-expected costs for some government programs. MPR News host Emily Bright spoke with state Sens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Biden admin extends $10B Iran sanctions waiver 2 days after Trump election win
item.description: The Biden administration has extended a sanctions waiver that allows Iran to access $10 billion in funds from Iraq – a move Republicans say frees up Iran to fund terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he'd do no such thing.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top DOGE senator demands answers on plan to exhaust CHIPs Act funds before Trump arrives
item.description: Senate DOGE Caucus leader Joni Ernst of Iowa is sending letter to Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo about her recent comments outlining how CHIPs Act funds will be spent.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State agency to ask lawmakers for $300 million to fix “significant neglect” in Texas’ Medicaid enrollment system
item.description: The request, which includes hiring more staff and updating the online application system, comes as applicants must wait for months to receive approval for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford, KPRC 2

item.title: James Comer predicts Biden will pardon his brother Jim
item.description: “I do think he’ll pardon other family members. He’s going to have to,” Comer said in the clip highlighted by Grabien.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department ignored some policies when seizing reporters' phone records, watchdog finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal prosecutors sidestepped some Justice Department rules when they seized the phone records or emails of reporters as part of media leak investigations during the Trump administration, according to a new watchdog report being released as the aggressive practice of hunting for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Yorkers could receive 'inflation refund' check from state: Here's how much
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of New Yorkers would receive hundreds of dollars in an “inflation refund” payment from the state next year under a new proposal from Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: McConnell receives medical attention after falling
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) received medical attention after falling following a Republican lunch meeting Tuesday. A spokesman for McConnell said the GOP leader tripped after the lunch. "He sustained a minor cut to the face and sprained his wrist.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House panel holds drone hearing
item.description: Capitol Hill has taken notice of recent, mysterious drone sightings across the Northeast and is hoping to get to the bottom of the mystery.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-era Justice Department subpoenaed congressional staffers, watchdog finds
item.description: But the department's internal watchdog found no evidence of political motivation by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Bragg lays out alternatives to dismissing Trump's hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s (D) office opposes dismissing President-elect Trump’s hush money charges but would accept halting the proceedings while he is in office, according to court documents made public Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi says she backs generational change on committees, but there are exceptions
item.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) said this week that she supports the push for younger Democrats to replace veteran lawmakers at the top of committees — but there is at least one glaring exception to the rule.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Biden to tout economic legacy in Tuesday speech
item.description: President Biden will deliver remarks Tuesday afternoon touting his economic agenda and making the case that it has been more effective than the kinds of tax cuts likely to be implemented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans pick Mast to lead House Foreign Affairs panel
item.description: House Republicans selected Rep. Brian Mast (R-Fla.) to chair the House Foreign Affairs Committee in the 119th Congress, sources told The Hill, as the conference works to organize its panels for the next two years of Republican-controlled Washington Republicans also selected Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bidders are back in court battling over the auction of Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: A U.S. bankruptcy judge is hearing arguments for and against selling the show to The Onion, the satirical news site named the winning bidder. Host Alex Jones says the auction was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard heads to the Hill with Syria in spotlight
item.description: Gabbard — who served in the House for eight years as a Democrat — has drawn scrutiny for a 2017 trip she made to Syria to visit with Bashar al-Assad.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Boston school admissions policy challenge as Alito, Thomas dissent
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a group of white and Asian parents’ challenge to a temporary admissions policy at Boston’s prestigious “exam schools” over dissents from two of the court’s conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News
